investments.headline: TRICAPITAL Angels leads £1.2m investment into sovereign dual use 5G solutions
investments.summary: JET Connectivity, a UK-developed technology company delivering ultra-reliable, infrastructure-independent 5G connectivity to hard-to-reach locations, secured a £1.2m investment led by Tricapital Angels. The funding will accelerate growth at a time of increasing global focus on national resilience and secure communications infrastructure. JET Connectivity provides land and sea-based alternatives to satellite networks for offshore, coastal and ultra-remote sites.
investments.raw_snippet: A pioneering UK-developed technology delivering ultra-reliable, infrastructure-independent 5G connectivity to the world's hardest-to-reach locations has secured a £1.2million investment to accelerate its growth. JET Connectivity has developed land and sea-based alternatives to satellite networks such as Starlink and brings secure, high-bandwidth communications to offshore, coastal and ultra-remote sites anywhere on the planet.

investments.headline: Lentitek raises £700k for cancer treatment technology
investments.summary: Edinburgh biotech Lentitek secured £700,000 from Equity Gap, the Scottish angel syndicate, to accelerate its next-generation cancer treatments. The round brings total investment to £1m in the last six months, following a £300,000 Innovate UK grant. Funding was also provided by Gabriel-IS, Apollo Informal Investment, Scottish Enterprise and prominent private investors.
investments.raw_snippet: Biotech company Lentitek has secured £700,000 from Equity Gap, the Scottish angel syndicate, to accelerate its next-generation cancer treatments. It brings total investment in the Edinburgh business to £1 million in the last six months, following a £300,000 Innovate UK grant.

investments.headline: Healthtech firm Bioliberty to accelerate international growth with new investment
investments.summary: Healthtech firm Bioliberty secured £7.7m in total funding over the last 10 months, including a £6.2m Series A led by £3m from the Scottish National Investment Bank alongside existing investors Archangels, Eos Advisory, Old College Capital, and Hanna Capital SEZC, plus new investor Conduit Connect. The full Series A round is expected to close later this year. This follows a £1.5m pre-Series A round in mid-2025 from the existing investor group.
investments.raw_snippet: Healthtech firm, Bioliberty, has secured a total of £7.7m of funding over the last 10 months. The most recent £6.2m Series A investment was led by £3m from the Scottish National Investment Bank, alongside participation from existing investors Archangels, Eos Advisory, Old College Capital, and Hanna Capital SEZC, as well as new investor Conduit Connect.

investments.headline: Earth Blox secures £6m to help businesses and banks finance the transition to a nature-positive economy
investments.summary: Edinburgh-based Earth Blox, which helps organisations understand how nature loss and climate hazards affect financial performance, secured £6m in new investment led by PXN Ventures with support from Scottish Enterprise and follow-on from Archangels and funding from the European Space Agency. The investment comes as banks and businesses face growing pressure to understand environmental risks affecting assets and supply chains.
investments.raw_snippet: Earth Blox, an Edinburgh-based technology company helping organisations understand how nature loss and climate hazards affect financial performance, has secured £6 million in new investment led by PXN Ventures with support from Scottish Enterprise, follow-on investment from Archangels and funding from the European Space Agency (ESA).

investments.headline: Scottish quantum hardware company raises £2.5m to build UK's sovereign manufacturing capability
investments.summary: Quantcore, a University of Glasgow spin-out, raised £2.5m in seed funding to create a sovereign supply chain for quantum hardware as the UK races to build domestic capacity in critical technologies. The round was co-led by PXN Ventures, Blackfinch Ventures and Scottish Enterprise, with investment also from Quantum Exponential and STAC. Quantcore plans to grow its team from four to 12 employees over the next 18 months.
investments.raw_snippet: Quantcore, a University of Glasgow spin-out, has raised £2.5m in seed funding to create a sovereign supply chain for quantum hardware, as the UK races to build domestic capacity in technologies critical to national security and economic competitiveness. The round was co-led by PXN Ventures, Blackfinch Ventures and Scottish Enterprise, with investment also coming from Quantum Exponential and STAC.

investments.headline: Edinburgh Uni spin-out at cutting edge of cancer research gets £1.6m injection
investments.summary: BIOCAPTIVA, a Scottish biotech behind a platform for early diagnosis and monitoring of difficult-to-detect cancers, raised £1.6m in a fresh funding round and launched its first product in the US. The round was led by existing investor Archangels with support from Old College Capital, BBI, Scottish Enterprise and new investor EverQuest Capital Partners.
investments.raw_snippet: A Scottish biotech firm behind a platform that could revolutionise the early diagnosis and monitoring of difficult-to-detect cancers has raised £1.6 million in a fresh funding round and launched its first product in the US. Its latest financing round was led by existing investor Archangels, with support from Old College Capital, BBI, Scottish Enterprise and new investor EverQuest Capital Partners.

investments.headline: Launchpad announces PXN Ventures as its newest strategic investor
investments.summary: Launchpad, an AI-first robotics company powering real-world assembly automation, announced PXN Ventures as a new strategic investor, adding to its oversubscribed $11m Series A fundraising announced in October 2025. PXN Ventures forms part of PXN Group with £700m AUM across over 120 portfolio companies following its launch in November 2025 after the merger of Par Equity and Praetura Ventures.
investments.raw_snippet: Launchpad, the AI-first robotics company powering real-world assembly automation, today announced PXN Ventures as a new investor. This builds on the company's oversubscribed, $11M Series A fundraising, announced in October 2025.

investments.headline: Bioliberty Announces $10.5 million Series A Financing to Build Functional Intelligence Capabilities for Post-Acute Care
investments.summary: Edinburgh- and Boston-based Bioliberty raised $10.5 million in a Series A led by the Scottish National Investment Bank. The funds will expand the company's Lifehub Clinic platform — which uses AI and robotics to monitor functional recovery in post-acute care settings — and commercialise Lifehub Home for in-home physical therapy tracking. The round brings total funding to $17.5 million.
investments.raw_snippet: Bioliberty is an Edinburgh- and Boston-based startup... Participating in the round were new investors including lead investor The Scottish National Investment Bank and Conduit Connect. Returning investors Archangel Investors, Eos Advisory, Hanna Capital, and Old College Capital also participated.

investments.headline: BIOCAPTIVA secures £1.58M to transform how blood samples are prepared for liquid biopsy
investments.summary: BIOCAPTIVA, headquartered at Roslin Innovation Centre in Midlothian, raised £1.58 million led by Archangels. The funding will support R&D to expand its msX magnetic bead platform — which extracts cell-free DNA from whole blood without centrifuges — and has enabled the company to launch its first product in the US market.
investments.raw_snippet: The raise was led by existing investor Archangels, with support from Old College Capital, BBI, Scottish Enterprise, and new investor EverQuest Capital Partners... BIOCAPTIVA has raised £1.58 million in a new funding round and launched its first product in the US.

Funding round worth £200k to help Scottish startup make Edinburgh most flexible working capital
investments.summary: Edinburgh-based remote working platform SWURF secured £200,000 from angel investors including Skyscanner co-founder Gareth Williams and hospitality entrepreneur Anna Lagerqvist Christopherson. The funding will support rollout of SWURF Pods — private soundproofed meeting spaces already installed at Edinburgh Airport and YOTEL — and expand the platform's 450+ venue and 14,000 active user network.
investments.raw_snippet: Edinburgh-based remote working platform SWURF has secured £200,000 in new funding. Investors include Gareth Williams (Skyscanner co-founder) and Anna Lagerqvist Christopherson (hospitality entrepreneur). The investment will primarily support rollout of SWURF Pods.

investments.headline: Spinout Exergy3 raises £10m to decarbonise industrial heat
investments.summary: Exergy3, a University of Edinburgh spinout, raised £10 million (€11.4m) in seed funding to commercialise its thermal energy storage technology that converts excess renewable electricity into high-temperature industrial heat. The round was led by Axeleo Capital's Article 9 Green Tech fund alongside Bayern Kapital and Kibo Invest, with support from Scottish Enterprise, Zero Carbon Capital, and Old College Capital. The company has already piloted its system at Annandale Distillery in southwest Scotland.
investments.raw_snippet: Exergy3, an Edinburgh-based CleanTech startup specialising in ultra-high temperature thermal energy storage solutions, has raised €11.4 million (£10 million) in Seed funding to scale its proprietary technology, addressing industrial decarbonisation, grid balancing and energy security. Lead investors: Axeleo Capital (Article 9 Green Tech Industry I fund), Bayern Capital, Kibo Invest. Follow-on investors: Scottish Enterprise, Zero Carbon Capital, Old College Capital.

investments.headline: Earth Blox secures £6m to finance the transition to a nature-positive economy
investments.summary: Edinburgh-based Earth Blox secured £6 million led by PXN Ventures to help organisations understand how nature loss and climate hazards affect financial performance. The round also includes follow-on investment from Archangels, support from Scottish Enterprise, and funding from the European Space Agency. The platform combines satellite, environmental, and portfolio data with AI-powered analysis for global banks and businesses.
investments.raw_snippet: Earth Blox, an Edinburgh-based technology company helping organisations understand how nature loss and climate hazards affect financial performance, has secured £6 million in new investment led by PXN Ventures with support from Scottish Enterprise, follow-on investment from Archangels and funding from the European Space Agency (ESA).

investments.headline: Tricapital: Scots syndicate leads £3m-plus deals and unveils key hire
investments.summary: Scottish angel syndicate Tricapital invested £506,000 in HonuWorx, a subsea robotics specialist. The deal forms part of a batch of investments totalling more than £3 million by Tricapital, whose members have invested £6.8m in Scottish businesses over two and a half years, matched by Scottish Enterprise. HonuWorx recently secured its first NATO contract through the defence innovation accelerator for the North Atlantic programme.
investments.raw_snippet: The latest investments involve £506,000 of backing for subsea robotics specialist HonuWorx... Angel syndicate Tricapital has led a series of fresh investments totalling more than £3 million as it focuses on critical areas such as the industrial transition and ocean technology.

investments.headline: Tricapital: Scots syndicate leads £3m-plus deals and unveils key hire
investments.summary: Tricapital invested £430,000 in Kaly, a sustainable seaweed business expanding its island-based seaweed supply chain. The investment is part of Tricapital's wider £3m-plus investment batch focused on the industrial transition and ocean technology sectors in Scotland.
investments.raw_snippet: £430,000 for sustainable seaweed business Kaly... Kaly expands its island-based seaweed supply chain.

investments.headline: Tricapital: Scots syndicate leads £3m-plus deals and unveils key hire
investments.summary: Tricapital invested £620,000 in Sisaltech, a low-carbon insulation manufacturer scaling production of natural fibre insulation products. The deal is part of Tricapital's latest batch of investments totalling over £3 million in early-stage Scottish businesses.
investments.raw_snippet: £620,000 for low-carbon insulation manufacturer Sisaltech... Sisaltech continues to scale production of natural fibre insulation products.
